sources.volume: LTA DataMall passenger volume by bus stop — weekday daily tap-ins (v2 bake, Jul 2026). Hourly dayparts pending DataMall account key.
sources.subzone: URA Master Plan 2019 subzone boundaries (data.gov.sg d_8594ae9ff96d0c708bc2af633048edfb)
sources.census: SingStat Census of Population 2020: age (d_d95ae740c0f8961a0b10435836660ce0), dwelling (d_7f243956483d5901f237e6f87b096636), ethnic group (d_e7ae90176a68945837ad67892b898466)
sources.movers: HDB resale txns 12mo to 2026-07 (d_8b84c4ee58e3cfc0ece0d773c8ca6abc), planning-area level
sources.daytime: Census 2020 employed residents by workplace planning area (d_be89529e906103da82ff06adec019f17)

assumptions: Equal-share allocation: a stop's tap-ins are split equally across all services calling there (frequency data not held; OD-constrained allocation planned).
Residence join: boarders are profiled as residents of the stop's subzone. This is the model's weakest link.
No ride-length factor yet: the unit is target-audience boardings per weekday, not impressions.
Indices capped at 300 (x3 national); capped contribution is tracked and widens the confidence label.
